target.title: [codex] Use red background for Sessions
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Sessions view used the same neutral page canvas as the rest of the Control UI, instead of the requested red background.

## Why This Change Was Made

- Add a route-specific `content--sessions` marker.
- Use a deep red derived from the existing danger token so light and dark themes stay consistent.
- Keep the page heading opaque and high-contrast while preserving the existing card and table surfaces.
- Cover the route marker with a focused render regression test.

## User Impact

The Sessions page now has a clearly red canvas without reducing table readability or changing any session behavior.

## Evidence

- `pnpm format:check ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/styles/layout.css ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`
- AWS Crabbox `cbx_fc2dc9150f68`, run `run_0bce655dac8d`: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts` — 21 tests passed.
- Visible WebVNC browser proof at 1920×1080 with the mock Sessions UI loaded at `/sessions`.
- Final Codex autoreview: no accepted/actionable findings.
